channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Little Nightmares VR: Altered Echoes Launches in April
item.content:encoded:
Little Nightmares VR: Altered Echoes has a release date. During the Spring Showcase at the Future Games Show, a trailer for the VR version of the Little Nightmares series was shown.

At the end of the trailer, it was announced that the game will be released on April 24. It is now available to wishlist on Steam. The game will also be available on PlayStation VR2 and Meta Quest.

More on Little Nightmares VR: Altered Echoes

The game is being published by Bandai Namco and developed by Iconik. Bandai Namco has published all of the other games in the franchise. The first two were developed by Tarsier Studios. Little Nightmares 3, however, was developed by Supermassive Games. Iconik’s only other release is the VR game King Pong in 2021.

“For the first time, experience the disturbing universe of Little Nightmares in first person, through your own eyes, in VR. Every sight, sound, and shadow becomes part of your reality, drawing you deeper into a twisted world where nightmare and truth blur. Each interaction with your surroundings reveals intricate puzzles, hidden paths, and secrets buried in darkness,” reads the game’s synopsis on Steam.

Little Nightmares 3 was released in October 2025. You can check out our review of the game here.

In other franchise-related news, the former developers of Little Nightmares are now working on a game that merges twin stick shooters with Metroidvanias. Learn more here.

Would you play Little Nightmares VR: Altered Echoes? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Jeff Kaplan Reveals His Breaking Point at Blizzard: It Was the Biggest ‘F You’
item.content:encoded:
Jeff Kaplan has explained what his breaking point was while working at Blizzard.

Jeff Kaplan recently participated in a lengthy interview on the Lex Fridman Podcast. During the discussion, he explained that his breaking point at Blizzard came during a conversation with the company’s CFO about Overwatch’s success.

Jeff Kaplan Had the Fate of 1,000 People in His Hands

Kaplan recalls being told several years ago that the game had to make a certain amount of money. The exact amount was bleeped out out of respect for his confidentiality agreement with Blizzard. There was also a recurring Revenue amount that was bleeped out. He was also told that if those metrics weren’t met, they would lay off 1,000 people and that it would be on Kaplan’s head.

Kaplan, who did end up leaving Activision Blizzard in 2021, was a co-creator of Overwatch and called that moment the biggest “fuck you” of his career. He would also say that the executive is no longer working at the company. Dennis Durkin, although unnamed, was the CFO of Activision Blizzard at the time that Kaplan is referring to.

Overwatch’s Recent Changes

After re-releasing as a sequel in 2023, Overwatch 2 reverted to its original name and is now taking a different narrative-driven approach. Blizzard has insisted that the name change does not mean that the original switch was a failure. Learn more about the change here.

Right now, the gaming industry is undergoing massive restructures in big and small companies. Perhaps most notable was Highguard, a highly touted game that had its servers go offline less than 2 months after starting. Learn more about that here.

How do you feel about this interaction? Do you think that is a justifiable breaking point? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP
